title: HN Who is Hiring Scraper
description: Scrape and parse jobs from Hacker News 'Who is Hiring?' monthly threads into structured data.

months.title: Months to Scrape
months.description:
Number of recent monthly 'Who is Hiring?' threads to scrape.

Each month typically has 500-800 job posts. Default: 2 (current + previous month).
months.sectionDescription: Control how many threads to scrape and how fast.

concurrency.title: Concurrency
concurrency.description: Number of parallel API requests for fetching HN comments. Higher = faster but more memory. Recommended: 10.

remoteOnly.title: Remote Jobs Only
remoteOnly.description: Only return jobs that mention remote work.
remoteOnly.sectionDescription: Filter jobs by location, keywords, or technologies. All filters use case-insensitive regex.

locationFilter.title: Location Filter
locationFilter.description:
Regex pattern to match job locations.

Examples:
- 'europe|germany|uk|france' -- European jobs
- 'remote' -- Any location containing 'remote'
- 'new york|san francisco|austin' -- US tech hubs
- 'prague|czech' -- Specific city/country

keywordFilter.title: Title/Keyword Filter
keywordFilter.description:
Regex pattern to match job titles or descriptions.

Examples:
- 'senior|staff|lead|principal' -- Senior roles
- 'full.?stack|fullstack' -- Full-stack engineers
- 'react|typescript|node' -- Tech-specific
- 'founding|head.of|vp|director' -- Leadership

technologyFilter.title: Technology Filter
technologyFilter.description:
Regex pattern to match technologies.

Examples:
- 'react|vue|angular' -- Frontend frameworks
- 'python|rust|go' -- Languages
- 'aws|gcp|azure' -- Cloud providers
- 'postgres|mongodb|redis' -- Databases

includeRawHtml.title: Include Raw HTML
includeRawHtml.description: Include the raw HTML from each HN comment in the output. Increases dataset size. Default: off to keep results lean.

outputFormat.title: Output Format
outputFormat.description:
How to format output records:
- 'unified' (default) — Normalized schema consistent across all ATS platforms with structured location, salary, company objects
- 'raw' — Native HN format, no normalization
- 'both' — Unified schema with original HN data in the 'raw' field
